---
title: "04 — Episode Cost Assembly"
description: |
  Assembles the full liver biopsy episode cost per hospital using the
  reliability-filtered charge data, with component breakdown and
  sensitivity analysis across charge types.
dependencies:
  - data/intermediate/medstar_charges.parquet
  - data/intermediate/code_reliability.parquet
  - data/intermediate/episode_codes.parquet
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

## Load Data

```{python}
import polars as pl
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from _common import INTERMEDIATE_DIR, CHARGE_COLS, EPISODE_FREQUENCIES, CPT_DESCRIPTIONS

medstar = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "medstar_charges.parquet")
reliability = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "code_reliability.parquet")
episode_codes = pl.read_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "episode_codes.parquet")

medstar_f = medstar.with_columns([
    pl.col(c).cast(pl.Float64, strict=False) for c in CHARGE_COLS
])

freq_df = pl.DataFrame([
    {"key": k, "cpt_code": str(v["code"]), "qty": v["qty"]}
    for k, v in EPISODE_FREQUENCIES.items()
])
print(f"Charges: {medstar_f.shape[0]:,} rows, {freq_df.shape[0]} episode components")
```

## Per-Hospital Component Coverage

Not every hospital lists charges for every episode code. Under the CMS
price transparency rule, hospitals are only required to publish charges for
services they actually provide. A community hospital that does not perform
liver biopsies or celiac plexus nerve blocks will not list those codes —
this reflects scope of services, not a disclosure gap.

```{python}
hospitals = sorted(medstar_f["hosp_name"].unique().to_list())
components = freq_df["key"].to_list()
codes = freq_df["cpt_code"].to_list()

matrix = []
for hosp in hospitals:
    hosp_data = medstar_f.filter(pl.col("hosp_name") == hosp)
    row = []
    for code in codes:
        row.append(1 if hosp_data.filter(pl.col("cpt_code") == code).shape[0] > 0 else 0)
    matrix.append(row)

hover_text = [
    [f"<b>{hosp}</b><br>{comp} (CPT {code})<br>{CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(code, '')}<br>{'Available' if matrix[i][j] else 'Not offered'}"
     for j, (comp, code) in enumerate(zip(components, codes))]
    for i, hosp in enumerate(hospitals)
]

fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=matrix,
    x=components,
    y=hospitals,
    colorscale=[[0, "#d32f2f"], [1, "#388e3c"]],
    showscale=False,
    hovertext=hover_text,
    hoverinfo="text",
    xgap=3,
    ygap=3,
))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Episode Component Coverage by Hospital",
    xaxis=dict(tickangle=-45, tickfont=dict(size=9)),
    yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=9)),
    height=400,
    margin=dict(b=100),
)
fig.show()
```

Most MedStar hospitals cover only the general components (office visits,
infusion). Only the larger academic/specialty centers — Georgetown,
Washington Hospital Center, and National Rehab — offer enough of the
episode to produce a meaningful total. The cost figures below should be
interpreted accordingly: low totals reflect a narrower scope of services,
not lower prices.

## Episode Cost Buildup

For each episode component, we take the median charge across all hospitals
(using the recommended charge type from the reliability analysis) and
multiply by the episode frequency.

```{python}
reliable_codes = reliability.filter(pl.col("is_reliable"))
episode_bundle = freq_df.join(
    reliable_codes.select(["cpt_code", "recommended_charge_type"]),
    on="cpt_code",
    how="left",
).with_columns(
    pl.col("recommended_charge_type").fill_null("charge_gross")
)

role_map = dict(zip(
    episode_codes["cpt_code"].to_list(),
    episode_codes["clinical_role"].to_list()
))

role_costs = []
for row in episode_bundle.iter_rows(named=True):
    code = row["cpt_code"]
    charge_col = row["recommended_charge_type"]
    code_data = medstar_f.filter(pl.col("cpt_code") == code)
    median = code_data[charge_col].drop_nulls().median() if code_data.shape[0] > 0 else None
    role_costs.append({
        "key": row["key"],
        "cpt_code": code,
        "clinical_role": role_map.get(code, "unknown"),
        "median_charge": median,
        "qty": row["qty"],
        "line_total": round(median * row["qty"], 2) if median else None,
    })

role_df = pl.DataFrame(role_costs).filter(pl.col("line_total").is_not_null())

sorted_roles = role_df.sort("line_total", descending=True)
labels = sorted_roles["key"].to_list()
values = sorted_roles["line_total"].to_list()
descs = [CPT_DESCRIPTIONS.get(c, "") for c in sorted_roles["cpt_code"].to_list()]

fig = go.Figure(go.Waterfall(
    x=labels + ["Episode Total"],
    y=values + [0],
    measure=["relative"] * len(values) + ["total"],
    text=[f"${v:,.0f}" for v in values] + [f"${sum(values):,.0f}"],
    textposition="outside",
    connector=dict(line=dict(color="#bdbdbd")),
    increasing=dict(marker=dict(color="#1976d2")),
    totals=dict(marker=dict(color="#7b1fa2")),
    customdata=descs + ["All components"],
    hovertemplate="<b>%{x}</b><br>%{customdata}<br>$%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.update_layout(
    title="Episode Cost Buildup by Component",
    yaxis_title="Cost ($)",
    xaxis=dict(tickangle=-45, tickfont=dict(size=9)),
    height=450,
    margin=dict(b=120),
    showlegend=False,
)
fig.show()
```

## Per-Hospital Comparison

How does the episode total break down per hospital? Each stacked segment
is one episode component — hospitals with missing segments don't offer
that service.

```{python}
episode_costs = []

for hosp in medstar_f["hosp_name"].unique().sort().to_list():
    hosp_data = medstar_f.filter(pl.col("hosp_name") == hosp)
    total = 0.0
    comps = []

    for row in episode_bundle.iter_rows(named=True):
        code = row["cpt_code"]
        qty = row["qty"]
        charge_col = row["recommended_charge_type"]

        code_data = hosp_data.filter(pl.col("cpt_code") == code)
        if code_data.shape[0] == 0:
            comps.append({"code": code, "key": row["key"], "charge": None, "qty": qty})
            continue

        median_charge = code_data[charge_col].drop_nulls().median()
        if median_charge is None:
            median_charge = code_data["charge_gross"].drop_nulls().median()

        cost = (median_charge or 0) * qty
        total += cost
        comps.append({"code": code, "key": row["key"], "charge": median_charge, "qty": qty})

    episode_costs.append({
        "hosp_name": hosp,
        "episode_total": round(total, 2),
        **{c["key"]: round(c["charge"] * c["qty"], 2) if c["charge"] else None for c in comps},
    })

episode_df = pl.DataFrame(episode_costs)

component_keys = [r["key"] for r in episode_bundle.iter_rows(named=True)]
hosp_names = episode_df.sort("episode_total")["hosp_name"].to_list()

fig = go.Figure()
for key in component_keys:
    if key not in episode_df.columns:
        continue
    vals = []
    for hosp in hosp_names:
        row = episode_df.filter(pl.col("hosp_name") == hosp)
        v = row[key][0] if row.shape[0] > 0 else None
        vals.append(v if v is not None else 0)
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=key,
        y=hosp_names,
        x=vals,
        orientation="h",
        hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>" + key + ": $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    title="Episode Cost Components by Hospital",
    xaxis_title="Cost ($)",
    height=450,
    legend=dict(font=dict(size=9), orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    margin=dict(l=160),
)
fig.show()
```

## Sensitivity: Gross vs. Discounted Charges

How much does the episode cost change when using gross charges vs.
discounted cash charges? Sorted by the largest differential.

```{python}
sensitivity = []

for charge_type in ["charge_gross", "charge_discounted"]:
    for hosp in medstar_f["hosp_name"].unique().sort().to_list():
        hosp_data = medstar_f.filter(pl.col("hosp_name") == hosp)
        total = 0.0
        for row in freq_df.iter_rows(named=True):
            code = row["cpt_code"]
            code_data = hosp_data.filter(pl.col("cpt_code") == code)
            median = code_data[charge_type].drop_nulls().median() if code_data.shape[0] > 0 else None
            total += (median or 0) * row["qty"]
        sensitivity.append({
            "hosp_name": hosp,
            "charge_type": charge_type,
            "episode_total": round(total, 2),
        })

sens_df = pl.DataFrame(sensitivity)
sens_pivot = (
    sens_df.pivot(on="charge_type", index="hosp_name", values="episode_total")
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("charge_gross") - pl.col("charge_discounted")).round(2).alias("difference"),
    )
    .sort("difference", descending=True)
)

hosp_sorted = sens_pivot.sort("difference")["hosp_name"].to_list()
gross_vals = [
    sens_df.filter((pl.col("hosp_name") == h) & (pl.col("charge_type") == "charge_gross"))["episode_total"][0]
    for h in hosp_sorted
]
disc_vals = [
    sens_df.filter((pl.col("hosp_name") == h) & (pl.col("charge_type") == "charge_discounted"))["episode_total"][0]
    for h in hosp_sorted
]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    name="Gross", y=hosp_sorted, x=gross_vals, orientation="h",
    marker_color="#1976d2",
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>Gross: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    name="Discounted", y=hosp_sorted, x=disc_vals, orientation="h",
    marker_color="#388e3c",
    hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>Discounted: $%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="group",
    title="Episode Cost: Gross vs. Discounted Charges",
    xaxis_title="Episode Total ($)",
    height=450,
    margin=dict(l=160),
)
fig.show()
```

## Episode Cost Summary

```{python}
sorted_costs = episode_df.sort("episode_total")
hosps = sorted_costs["hosp_name"].to_list()
totals = sorted_costs["episode_total"].to_list()
median_val = sorted(totals)[len(totals) // 2]

fig = go.Figure()
for h, t in zip(hosps, totals):
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[0, t], y=[h, h], mode="lines", line=dict(color="#bdbdbd", width=2),
        showlegend=False, hoverinfo="skip",
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=[t], y=[h], mode="markers",
        marker=dict(size=12, color="#1976d2"),
        showlegend=False,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>$%{x:,.0f}<extra></extra>",
    ))

fig.add_vline(x=median_val, line_dash="dash", line_color="#7b1fa2",
              annotation_text=f"Median ${median_val:,.0f}")
fig.update_layout(
    title="Liver Biopsy Episode Cost Across MedStar Hospitals",
    xaxis_title="Episode Total ($)",
    height=400,
    margin=dict(l=160),
)
fig.show()
```

## Write Episode Costs

```{python}
episode_df.write_parquet(INTERMEDIATE_DIR / "episode_costs.parquet")
print(f"Wrote episode_costs.parquet: {episode_df.shape[0]} rows")
```
